name: General Data Protection Regulation
description: EU-wide data protection and privacy regulation governing processing of personal data of individuals in the EEA. Rackspace Technology acts as data processor under GDPR for customer data hosted in EU data centers. Data Processing Addendum with Standard Contractual Clauses incorporated in Master Services Agreement. com/gdpr, 10-K FY2024.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Major privacy framework baseline ($2.5M) + max penalty = 4% global revenue per GDPR Article 83

name: Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
description: US federal statute requiring public companies to maintain internal controls over financial reporting with CEO/CFO certification. Rackspace Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: RXT, CIK 0001810019) is Delaware-incorporated and NASDAQ-listed, subject to SOX Sections 302/404.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Public company reporting costs ($1.5M) + typical SOX civil penalty exposure

name: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 Reporting Requirements
description: US federal statute Sections 13/15(d) requiring public companies to file periodic reports (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K, proxy statements) with the SEC. Rackspace Technology subject as NASDAQ-listed registrant.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Securities compliance framework ($1.2M) + SEC enforcement action exposure

name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
description: US federal statute establishing national standards for protection of electronic protected health information (ePHI). Rackspace Technology provides HIPAA-compliant hosting environments as a business associate with BAA support across dedicated and cloud hosting. com/compliance, Carahsoft listing, 10-K FY2024.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Healthcare compliance framework ($1.8M) + HIPAA penalty per violation/case

name: Federal Information Security Modernization Act of 2014
description: US federal statute requiring federal agencies and their service providers to develop, document, and implement information security programs. Rackspace Technology holds FISMA High authorization via FedRAMP baseline. com/compliance/fedramp.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Government compliance baseline ($3.5M) + federal contract termination risk

name: International Traffic in Arms Regulations
description: US State Department regulations (codified 22 CFR 120-130) controlling export, import, and brokering of defense articles and technical data on the US Munitions List. Rackspace Government Solutions provides ITAR-compliant hosting within US-only environments. com/compliance/fedramp.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Export control framework ($2.5M) + ITAR criminal/civil penalty exposure ($25M)

name: UK Data Protection Act 2018 / UK GDPR
description: UK domestic implementation of GDPR principles post-Brexit via European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 and Data Protection Act 2018. Governs processing of personal data by UK-operating organizations. Applicable to Rackspace Limited (Co# 03897010) and UK data center infrastructure. com/gdpr, ICO guidance.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Major privacy framework baseline ($2.5M) + max penalty = 4% global revenue per GDPR Article 83

name: NIS2 Directive
description: EU directive (2022/2555) strengthening cybersecurity requirements for essential and important entities across 18 sectors including digital infrastructure and managed service providers. Requires risk management, incident reporting within 24 hours, management accountability. Applicable to Rackspace as digital infrastructure/MSP provider in EU. Member state transposition deadline October 17, 2024.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Cybersecurity framework baseline ($1.3M) + NIS directive penalty exposure

name: Digital Operational Resilience Act
description: EU regulation (2022/2554) establishing ICT risk management, incident reporting, resilience testing, and third-party oversight for financial entities and their critical ICT service providers. Applicable to Rackspace as ICT third-party service provider to EU financial sector customers.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Operational resilience framework ($1.1M) + regulatory penalty exposure

name: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
description: First comprehensive AI regulation (2024/1689) establishing risk-based framework with mandatory requirements for high-risk AI systems including risk management, data governance, transparency, and human oversight. Applicable to Rackspace as provider of AI services (Rackspace AI Anywhere) in EU market.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: AI compliance framework ($1M) + EU AI Act potential penalties (emerging)

name: China Personal Information Protection Law
description: Comprehensive data privacy law governing processing of personal information of individuals in China with strict cross-border transfer and data localization requirements. Applicable to Rackspace via Shanghai data center (SHA3).

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: China privacy compliance framework ($1.5M) + PIPL enforcement penalty exposure

name: China Cybersecurity Law
description: Chinese law establishing cybersecurity requirements including data localization, network security obligations, and critical information infrastructure protection for operators in mainland China.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: China cybersecurity framework ($1.8M) + cybersecurity law penalty exposure

name: India Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023
description: India's comprehensive digital privacy law establishing consent-driven, rights-based framework for digital personal data. Draft Rules published January 2025 by MeitY. Applicable to Rackspace via major delivery centers in Gurgaon (site-009), Hyderabad (site-010), and Bangalore (site-011).

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: India privacy framework ($1.2M) + DPDP Act penalty exposure

name: Singapore Personal Data Protection Act
description: Singapore data protection law governing collection, use, disclosure, and care of personal data. Applicable to Rackspace via Singapore APAC office (site-008, Just Analytics acquisition Jan 2022).

estimation_methodology: Singapore privacy compliance ($900K) + PDPA penalty exposure

name: Texas Identity Theft Enforcement and Protection Act
description: Texas breach notification statute (Bus. & Com. Code §521) requiring notification within 60 days of breach discovery, AG notification within 30 days if 250+ Texans affected. Civil penalties $2,000-$50,000 per violation, up to $250,000 for failure to notify. Rackspace HQ in San Antonio (site-001). Bus. & Com. Code Ann. §521.001 et seq., Texas AG.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Texas identity theft compliance ($400K) + state enforcement penalty

name: Texas Data Privacy and Security Act
description: Texas comprehensive consumer privacy law granting residents rights to access, correct, delete, and opt out of data processing/sale. Enforced by Texas AG with $7,500 per violation penalties. 30-day cure period. Applicable to Rackspace given Texas HQ and operations.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Texas data privacy framework ($1.2M) + TDPSA penalty exposure

name: California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act
description: California comprehensive privacy law granting consumers rights to know, delete, opt-out of sale/sharing, and limit use of sensitive personal information. Enforced by CPPA and California AG. Applicable to Rackspace given California data center operations (site-026 SJC3, site-027 SFO) and California-resident customer/employee data processing. Civ. Code §1798.100 et seq.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: California privacy compliance ($1.5M) + CCPA/CPRA penalty exposure ($7500 per violation)

name: Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act
description: Virginia comprehensive consumer data protection law effective January 1, 2023. Applicable to Rackspace given significant Virginia operational presence (site-002 Reston, site-023/024/025 Ashburn/Herndon DCs). Code Ann. §59.1-575 et seq.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Virginia privacy framework ($900K) + VCDPA penalty exposure

name: Delaware Personal Data Privacy Act
description: Delaware comprehensive privacy law effective January 1, 2025. Rackspace Technology, Inc. incorporated in Delaware. Applies to businesses processing personal data of 35,000+ Delaware consumers or 10,000+ consumers with revenue from data sales. Code Ann. tit. 6, ch. 12D.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Delaware privacy framework ($600K) + DPDPA penalty exposure

name: Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act
description: Illinois statute regulating collection, use, storage, and destruction of biometric identifiers and information. Private right of action with $1,000-$5,000 per violation statutory damages. Applicable to Rackspace given Chicago DC operations (site-028 ORD1) and potential employee/visitor biometric data collection.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Illinois biometric compliance ($700K) + BIPA penalty exposure per violation

name: Oregon Consumer Privacy Act
description: Oregon comprehensive privacy law effective July 1, 2024. Unique expanded definition of personal data including derived data. GLBA/HIPAA exemptions narrowly scope covered data, not entities. Applicable to Rackspace given Oregon DC presence (site-033 Hillsboro). Rev. Stat. §646A.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: Oregon privacy framework ($1.1M) + OCPA penalty exposure

name: New Jersey Data Privacy Act
description: New Jersey comprehensive privacy law effective January 15, 2025. Applies to controllers processing data of 100,000+ NJ consumers or 25,000+ with revenue from data sales. Applicable to Rackspace given NJ DC operations (site-030/031 NYC1/NYC2 Somerset). J.S.A. 56:8-166.1 et seq.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

estimation_methodology: New Jersey privacy framework ($800K) + NJDPA penalty exposure

name: Washington My Health My Data Act
description: Washington state health data privacy law extending protections beyond HIPAA to consumer health data held by non-HIPAA-covered entities. Geofencing provisions. Private right of action. Applicable to Rackspace given Washington DC operations (site-032 Tukwila). 373.

Rackspace directly subject as SEC registrant. 33-11216; 34-97989, effective December 18, 2023.

Rackspace maintains all three attestations. com/compliance/soc, 10-K FY2024.

name: EU-US Data Privacy Framework
description: Transatlantic data transfer mechanism replacing invalidated EU-US Privacy Shield. Enables certified US organizations to receive personal data from EU/EEA. Rackspace utilizes SCCs and DPF for lawful cross-border transfers. com/gdpr, EC adequacy decision July 10, 2023.

description: International quality management standard. Rackspace UK management, delivery, and support functions certified to ISO 9001. com/compliance/iso.

description: International OH&S management standard. Rackspace holds ISO 45001 certification (cert OHS 587454). com/compliance/iso.

description: Prioritized set of cybersecurity best practices (18 controls) published by Center for Internet Security. Widely adopted by managed service providers and referenced in multiple regulatory frameworks. Applicable to Rackspace as operational security baseline.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: CSA STAR / Cloud Controls Matrix
description: Cloud Security Alliance Security Trust Assurance and Risk program providing cloud-specific security assurance framework. CCM maps to ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI DSS, and FedRAMP. Applicable to Rackspace as multicloud managed service provider.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: ISO/IEC 27017 Cloud Security Controls
description: International standard providing cloud-specific guidance for information security controls extending ISO 27002. Provides implementation guidance for both CSPs and cloud customers. Applicable to Rackspace as multicloud service provider.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: ISO/IEC 27018 PII Protection in Public Cloud
description: International standard establishing commonly accepted control objectives and guidelines for protecting PII in cloud computing environments. Applicable to Rackspace as cloud service provider processing customer PII.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: ISO/IEC 27701 Privacy Information Management System
description: International standard extending ISO 27001/27002 for privacy information management. Provides framework for PII controllers and processors. Applicable to Rackspace as data processor handling customer PII globally.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Foreign Corrupt Practices Act
description: US federal statute (15 U.S.C. §78dd) prohibiting bribery of foreign government officials and requiring accurate books and records for SEC registrants. Anti-bribery provisions apply to all US persons and SEC registrants. Accounting provisions (Section 13(b)) require internal controls over financial reporting. Applicable to Rackspace as NASDAQ-listed registrant with operations in 15+ countries including India, UAE, Malaysia, Germany, Australia, Singapore, UK. S.C. §78dd-1 et seq., 10-K FY2024 global operations.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: UK Bribery Act 2010
description: UK statute establishing offences of bribing, being bribed, bribery of foreign public officials, and failure of commercial organisations to prevent bribery. Section 7 corporate offence of failing to prevent bribery has strict liability with 'adequate procedures' defence. Applicable to Rackspace Limited (Co# 03897010) and any UK-connected commercial organisation. 23, Rackspace UK operations.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Bank Secrecy Act / OFAC Sanctions Regulations
description: US federal anti-money laundering statute (31 U.S.C. §5311 et seq.) and OFAC sanctions regulations (31 CFR Part 500) prohibiting transactions with sanctioned persons, entities, and countries. Rackspace AUP explicitly prohibits services for entities involved in terrorism, narcotics trafficking, arms trafficking, or sanctions violations. S.C. §5311, 31 CFR Part 500, rackspace.com/en-nl/information/legal/global/aup Section 7.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
description: US federal statute (42 U.S.C. §2000e) prohibiting employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, and national origin. Applies to employers with 15+ employees. Rackspace employs approximately 9,600 people (10-K FY2024) with confirmed EEO commitments and 100% HRC Corporate Equality Index score. S.C. §2000e, 10-K FY2024 employee count, 2021 ESG Report.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Americans with Disabilities Act / Section 508 Rehabilitation Act
description: US federal statutes (42 U.S.C. §12101 / 29 U.S.C. §794d) prohibiting disability discrimination and requiring accessible electronic and information technology for federal agencies and their contractors. ADA Title III applies to public accommodations including websites. Section 508 applies to Rackspace Government Solutions as federal contractor.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Occupational Safety and Health Act (OSHA General Duty Clause)
description: US federal statute (29 U.S.C. §654) requiring employers to furnish employment free from recognized hazards causing or likely to cause death or serious physical harm. General Duty Clause applies to all Rackspace US facilities including data centers with electrical, mechanical, and environmental hazards. S.C. §654(a)(1), Rackspace US site operations.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: US Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (FASB ASC)
description: FASB Accounting Standards Codification governing financial reporting for US public companies. Rackspace applies ASC 606 (Revenue from Contracts with Customers) for multi-element service arrangements, ASC 842 (Leases), ASC 350 (Goodwill/Intangibles), and ASC 718 (Stock Compensation). SEC requires GAAP-compliant financial statements in 10-K/10-Q filings.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform Act Section 1502 (Conflict Minerals)
description: US federal statute requiring SEC registrants to disclose use of conflict minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold) originating from DRC or adjoining countries. Annual Form SD filing and Conflict Minerals Report required. Rackspace ESG report references supplier engagement on conflict minerals standards. 13p-1, rackspace.com/about/responsibility.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: CAN-SPAM Act
description: US federal statute (15 U.S.C. §7701) establishing requirements for commercial email messages including opt-out mechanisms, accurate header information, physical address inclusion, and prohibitions on deceptive subject lines. FTC enforcement with penalties up to $50,120 per violation. Rackspace AUP Section 4 incorporates email marketing compliance requirements including affirmative consent and opt-out honoring. S.C. §7701 et seq., rackspace.com/en-nl/information/legal/global/aup Section 4.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: Lanham Act (Trademark Act of 1946)
description: US federal trademark statute (15 U.S.C. §1051 et seq.) governing registration, protection, and enforcement of trademarks and service marks. Rackspace maintains registered trademarks including Rackspace®, Rackspace Technology®, and Fanatical Experience®. Published trademark notice at rackspace.com/information/legal. S.C. §1051, rackspace.com/information/legal (Copyright and Trademarks page).

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation

name: UK Finance Act 2016 Schedule 19 (Tax Strategy Publication)
description: UK statute requiring qualifying groups and companies to publish their tax strategy annually. Rackspace publishes UK Tax Strategy (156.4 KB PDF) on ir.rackspace.com as required. Applies to UK entities meeting revenue/balance sheet thresholds. rackspace.com/corporate-governance/documents-and-charters.

provenance.primary_data_source: Official regulatory publications, legal databases, compliance documentation
